rulings.Case Title: DAVID P. DINSLAGE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 25, 2014, Line 3. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DENNIS KERN, DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS, PHIL GINSBURG, AND RECREATION AND PARKS DEPARTMENT OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants' motion for summary judgment is granted. As to the age discrimination cause of action, Defendants have met their burden to show that Plaintiff cannot establish a prima facie case of age discrimination under the Fair Housing and Employment Act ("FEHA") because the Defendants neither acted with a discriminatory motive nor were the Defendants' legitimate business reasons for not rehiring the Plaintiff pretextual. Plaintiff fails to meet his shifting burden to show a triable issue of fact exists as to the Defendants' motive. Plaintiff does not present any evidence other than his own declaration that the Defendants' motive in not rehiring the Plaintiff was based on Plaintiff's age. As to the harassment cause of action under the FEHA, Defendants have met their burden to show that the behavior complained of by Plaintiff regarding Defendants Kern and Ginsburg was not based on Plaintiff's age or other protected characteristics, and the behavior was not sufficiently severe or pervasive to create a hostile work environment. Plaintiff fails to meet his shifting burden to show a triable issue of fact exists as to the individual Defendants' behavior because Plaintiff admits in his declaration and deposition that the individual Defendants behaved professionally in all their encounters while Plaintiff was employed. Plaintiff only presents legal conclusions that Defendant Kern targeted Plaintiff for termination and subsequently created a hostile work environment. As to the retaliation cause of action, Defendants have met their burden to show that Plaintiff did not engage in protected activity under the FEHA. Defendants' evidence shows that Plaintiff did not speak out against the Defendants for engaging in discriminatory conduct directed at Defendants' employees. Plaintiff fails to meet his shifting burden to show a triable issue of fact exists demonstrating that Plaintiff did engage in protected activity. Plaintiff's evidence does not show that the Plaintiff opposed employer conduct that was discriminatory toward employees but only shows that the Plaintiff spoke in public forums regarding his concern that the Recreation & Parks Department's reorganization would cause layoffs and the potential negative effects the reorganization would have on members of the public who have disabilities. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted pursuant to Evidence Code sections 452(b), 452(d), and 453. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARK BIRKHEAD VS. OLIVER PARKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel Further Response To Pltfs Request For Production Of Documents Set No. 1
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 24, 2014, line 1, PLAINTIFF MARK BIRKHEAD's Motion For Order To Compel Further Response To Pltfs Request For Production Of Documents Set No. 1 Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: :The court will refrain from issuing a ruling in this matter until the hearing on the merits. It does not appear to the court that the parties have fully exhausted their obligation to meet and confer about this matter. The parties are requested to hold an additional meet and confer session prior to the hearing, perhaps at 8:00 AM on Tuesday. The parties, through counsel, shall be prepared to report to the court at the hearing on the progress they have made on simplifying and reducing the items in dispute. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: RYAN GARMAN et al VS. UNIVERSITY PIPELINE, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 25, 2014, Line 19. DEFENDANTS TOM UNGER, STEPHANIE LOSEE'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the sixth through eleventh causes of action. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege each cause of action. For the seventh cause of action, although unjust enrichment is not a cognizable cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege restitution. For the breach of fiduciary claims, whether Defendant Unger had a fiduciary duty to or a special relationship with Plaintiff as a business partner, shareholder or trustee involves questions of fact that are not the subject of a demurrer. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIAN VILLANUEVA VS. BRENT PEARSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Christian Villanueva'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 25, 2014, Line 22. Plaintiff Christian Villanueva's Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint. Denied as to intentional tort cause of action. Defendant owes no duty to avoid spending money in order to satisfy a potential judgment in favor of Plaintiff. Further, allegations in the proposed new tort cause of action are insufficient to state a cause of action. As for the proposed new Plaintiff and her cause of action for loss of consortium, Defendant represented that Plaintiff has withdrawn this request. The Reply did not confirm this withdrawal nor did it discuss this new party and claim. If the loss of consortium claim is still requested, then a hearing is required. If not, then no hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 25, 2014, Line 17. DEFENDANTS PAMELA PALPALLATOC, JAMES GLEED, FRANCIS LAW, SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with 15 days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
